text:
You are a teammate on the swarm-wave team. Your task is:

**Task #1: [str-fpgb.13] Rust version check parity** (bug fix, P1)

The Rust frontend requires exact protocol-version equality, while the core and TypeScript frontend use a major/minor compatibility rule. This creates frontend-specific handshake behavior.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Add a failing automated test that captures the intended compatibility rule for the Rust handshake
- Verify it fails on current exact-match behavior
- Update the Rust frontend so version handling matches the canonical protocol rule

**Workflow:**
1. Mark task #1 as in_progress with TaskUpdate
2. `bd start str-fpgb.13` to create branch
3. Research how version checking works in shatter-core and shatter-ts (look at handshake handling)
4. Write a failing test first (per project policy)
5. Fix the Rust frontend version check to use major/minor compatibility
6. Run `cargo test` and `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` in shatter-rust
7. Commit changes on the branch
8. Mark task #1 as completed with TaskUpdate
9. Send a message to team-lead with results

{"type":"task_assignment","taskId":"1","subject":"[str-fpgb.13] Rust version check parity","description":"Bug fix: Rust frontend requires exact protocol-version equality while core/TS use major/minor compatibility. Add failing test capturing intended compatibility rule, update Rust frontend version handling. Branch: str-fpgb.13. See `bd show str-fpgb.13` for full details.","assignedBy":"proto-bugfix","timestamp":"2026-03-12T01:22:12.024Z"}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1773278732082-syoibj9","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"cd /home/ketan/project/shatter && git add shatter-rust/src/handler.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(shatter-rust): use major.minor version compatibility instead of exact match

The Rust frontend required exact protocol version equality during
handshake, while shatter-core, shatter-ts, and shatter-go all use
major.minor comparison (ignoring patch). This caused the Rust frontend
to reject valid patch-different versions like "0.1.1" when expecting
"0.1.0".

Add parse_major_minor() and is_version_compatible() matching the
Go/TypeScript pattern. Includes tests for patch-version acceptance
and malformed-version rejection.

Closes str-fpgb.13

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"","description":"Commit the version check parity fix"},"permission_updates":[]}}

{"type":"permission_response","request_id":"perm-1773278874749-xfvd12e","subtype":"success","response":{"updated_input":{"command":"git checkout str-fpgb.13 && git cherry-pick 20528e0","description":"Cherry-pick commit onto correct branch"},"permission_updates":[]}}

{"type":"shutdown_request","requestId":"shutdown-1773278997719@proto-bugfix","from":"team-lead","reason":"Great work on str-fpgb.13, all done. Shutting you down.","timestamp":"2026-03-12T01:29:57.719Z"}
